paper_title: Mixing properties of colorings of the $\mathbb{Z}^d$ lattice
summary: Theorem 1.2 of arXiv:1903.11685 establishes that $[n]^d$ is $L^d_n$-list-colorable whenever $n \geq d+2$, while the paper demonstrates non-list-colorability for $n < d+2$ in specific cases (e.g., $[2]^3$ is not $L^3_2$-list-colorable). The optimal threshold $n$ as a function of $d$ — that is, whether $n = d+2$ is always tight or whether improvements are possible for particular dimensions — remains an open question. No follow-up paper resolving this question was found in the published literature after the paper's posting.
search_queries: arXiv 1903.11685 list-colorable Z^d lattice colorings Chandgotia Spinka
list-colorability threshold lattice colorings mixing "d+2" Alon Briceño Chandgotia Magazinov Spinka
Chandgotia Spinka "list-colorable" lattice colorings optimal threshold follow-up 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024
notes: No follow-up found across 5 web calls (2 searches + 3 fetches). The conjecture is recent (posted 2019, published in Combinatorics, Probability and Computing 2021) and the question of optimality of the $n \geq d+2$ threshold is stated as an open problem in the paper itself (justified by Proposition 3.3). Absence of indexed follow-up supports high-confidence open status.
